behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser + downstream hook-input handling handoff, not just JSON.parse in isolation (MOCK-MISS). Candidate Unit test: call parse function with markdown string and assert parsed object; catches pure parsing bugs but can miss wiring errors where valid parse is later misrouted. Candidate Integration test: run in-process hook input pipeline (parser -> handler) with valid markdown-heavy JSON and assert normal handled path/trace; catches false attribution bugs in module handoff. No real OS/network/subprocess required (reject System by behavior text: 'local hook input handling'). No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Set up an in-process hook input pipeline with real parser and handler modules plus a test trace sink. Feed a valid JSON payload whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and `$vars`. Assert the request reaches the normal handled path (no parse-failure trace) and produces expected success-side handling metadata.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about parse error propagation into tracing with diagnostic context, which is a module interaction boundary (parser -> trace emitter). Unit-only parsing can miss missing/incorrect trace fields. Candidate Unit test: assert parser throws/returns syntax error; catches parser failure only. Candidate Integration test: malformed payload through local parse+trace path and assert `json_parse_failed` plus contextual snippet/offset fields; catches lost-context bugs. No real process requirement in text, so System is not required here.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser and trace recording components in-process. Provide malformed JSON (e.g., truncated object near markdown content) and assert one `json_parse_failed` trace record is emitted containing diagnostic context fields (such as location/snippet) that identify malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit local orchestration: parse failure object must map through error-policy into handled result and telemetry intent flag. Candidate Unit test: test policy mapper with synthetic error object; may miss real shape mismatches from parser boundary. Candidate Integration test: induce real parse failure in local pipeline and assert no throw + handled result + telemetry intent marker; catches contract drift between modules. Text says 'local error-policy path', disqualifying System; no AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run local parse->error-policy path with malformed JSON. Assert call returns handled result object (not exception), and telemetry intent marker is set as expected for parse failures.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real process semantics (stdin, crash/non-crash, before-exit signal), so REAL-INFRA applies. In-process integration fakes can hide buffering/exit-order/stdio edge failures (MOCK-HIDE). Candidate Integration test: invoke entry function with string input and assert trace emission; can miss subprocess exit/stdio timing issues. Candidate System test: spawn real hook process, write malformed stdin, assert non-crash exit behavior and expected trace signal emitted before termination.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook executable as a subprocess, send malformed JSON over stdin, and capture exit status plus emitted traces. Assert process does not hard-crash and that expected parse-failure trace signal is observed prior to process exit.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text names full real invocation path including stdin and real trace artifact emission, crossing process/IO boundaries. Candidate Integration test: call parser+sink directly and assert artifact object; misses invocation plumbing and actual artifact write path. Candidate System test: execute hook via real stdin path and verify emitted artifact in its real sink/output location. No LLM steps involved, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin and inspect the actual trace artifact output channel used in production. Assert artifact includes the expected `json_parse_failed` signal and is emitted on the real invocation path.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: This is a comparative regression across parser/error classification and tracing behavior for two input classes, requiring module wiring but not real OS. Candidate Unit test: parser-only cases for valid vs invalid JSON; can miss incorrect classification/trace mapping in higher layer. Candidate Integration test: run both payloads through local pipeline and assert only malformed case yields parse-failure classification/trace while valid markdown case follows normal path. System considered but rejected: behavior text is about attribution logic, not subprocess/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired regression cases in one in-process suite: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy content, (b) genuinely malformed JSON. Assert only case (b) emits parse-failure signal and case (a) follows normal handling, ensuring markdown content is not blamed.
